docs.title: Welcome to Adaptive Gaming
docs.text: 🖨️ Print Resource Guide

docs.title: Why Adaptive Gaming Matters
docs.text:
Gaming is the largest entertainment industry in the world, providing a vital space for entertainment, careers, and community. However, accessibility challenges often remain a barrier to full participation.

For the 31% of gamers who live with a disability, adaptive gaming is life-changing:

  • Rehabilitation: It gamifies physical and cognitive therapy sessions.
  • Social Connection: It reduces isolation by connecting players to a global community.
  • Independence: It increases autonomy through digital participation.

Makers Making Change works to ensure the disability community can fully participate by providing open-source assistive technology at about one-tenth the cost of similar commercial devices.

docs.title: Program Overview
docs.text: Scan to watch: Adaptive Gaming Overview Video

docs.title: Your Pathway to Play
docs.text:
Follow the belwo path to get a deep understanding of the equipment, video game literacy, and best practices around adaptive gaming:

  1. Adaptive Gaming Equipment: Explore Alternative Access, Controller Modifications, and Software Features.
  2. Video Game Basics: Learn How to Pick Games and build Video Game Literacy.
  3. Best Practices: Follow our Session Walkthrough for a successful assessment and setup. View Tips and Tricks that we have developed or learned through the years at Makers Making Change.
  4. Gamer Profiles: Check out the setups of various individuals to get insight into how this can apply to yourself or client.

docs.title: Other Resources found on this site
docs.text:
1. GAME Checkpoint Resources: Are you a centre that is looking for resources to help you serve your community? This section allows you to access Questionnaires, Equipment Lists, Marketing and Posters, and various Templates.
  2. Get Support: Check out this section to find out how to get in contact with us.
  3. Download/Print: Visit this page if you would like to print a static version (.PDF) of everything included on this site. The video and linked sections will be turned into QR codes.

Check out the official GAME Checkpoints Program.

Link: Visit the GAME Checkpoints Webpage

docs.title: About the Project
docs.text: This resource guide is an initiative developed by Makers Making Change, a program of Neil Squire.

docs.title: About Neil Squire
docs.text: “We use Technology, Knowledge, and Passion to Empower Canadians with Disabilities.” Neil Squire, the Canadian leader in assistive technology, has been revolutionizing the lives of Canadians with disabilities since 1984. Our work is focused on four distinct areas: Innovation, Digital Literacy, Employment, and Assistive Technology. Our work enables people with disabilities to achieve their goals, reach their full potential and continue living life to the fullest.

docs.title: About Makers Making Change
docs.text:
Makers Making Change is a program within the Neil Squire Society. Within this program, we have an assistive devices library where people can request devices. They then get matched with a volunteer maker who will work with the requester to build the device and customize anything they can on it if that is within the volunteer's skills (color, size, shape, etc). The requester will then pay the volunteer for only the cost of materials and shipping if applicable.

Our team at MMC also hosts plenty of events where we build various devices and have them in stock that we can give away for free if the materials were donated. So depending on the request, we can give devices away at no cost.

Visit Makers Making Change 🌐

docs.title: Equipment Lists
docs.text:
When we launch a GAME Checkpoint (adaptive gaming space) we start with a base list of adaptive gaming equipment. This document is the initial baseline that we usually start with to help a centre determine what they need. Of course, each centre is different, this is just a starting point.

docs.title: Pre Session Questionnaire
docs.text:
These adaptive gaming session questionnaires were created to help the GAME Checkpoint Leads collect the information prior to a gamers first session.

  • The goal is to understand the gamers goals, determine the type of AT to try in the first session, and to help document the process while working with a gamer.
  • The content in the questionnaire covers the gamers goals, physical and cognitive abilities, and environment/support information.

There are three documents you can download that are explained below:

Gamer Questionnaire - Insights

This document includes all of the questions for the users along with notes that MMC staff have added to give the GAME Checkpoint Lead more context to “why” the question is being asked.

Download Gamer Questionnaire - PDF Fillable

This document is intended to be sent directly to gamers or to be filled out. All of the content from the same questions from the insights document but without the notes for the GAME Checkpoint Lead.

Download Gamer Questionnaire (docx)

This is the Word doc version of the “PDF Fillable”. You can use this if you want to use the questionnaire as a template and change any information or if it is easier to fill out using Word.

Download

docs.title: Get Support
docs.text: Are you looking for support as either a clinician or a individual looking to get into adaptive gaming? Check out the list below.

docs.title: Templates
docs.text:
As we continue to make resources, we want to share the templated versions of them so you can expand on the work we have done to continue to advance accessibility. Currently the templates are:

  • Documenting Game Accessibility
Documenting Game Accessibility

This document is intended to help you document the key features of a video game such as the controls, accessibility features, and game modes. This will help you have a reference while creating an accessible gaming setup and prevent time repeativly looking up information on the game.

Download
